description: This figure contains two bright-field transmission electron microscopy micrographs showing typical dislocation structures of Fe-35at.%Al alloy with B2 ordered structure after 2-3% room temperature compression deformation. The left micrograph has a scale bar of 0.25 μm, and the higher magnification right micrograph has a scale bar of 0.1 μm. For both micrographs, the zone axis is near [1 1 0] and the imaging vector g (marked by the arrow) is 1 1 0 (barred). A large number of straight near-screw superdislocations and edge dipole segments are observed in both images; the right micrograph clearly shows that some dipole segments are dragged out of glissile screw dislocations when the dislocations encounter obstacles. These superdislocations are B2 lattice superdislocations composed of two 1/2<111> partial dislocations with small spacing due to high antiphase boundary fault energy. This figure provides direct microstructural evidence for the work hardening mechanism of high-Al Fe-Al alloys, confirming that dislocation dipole formation occurs during deformation.
